Skip to contents
aquacroptools 0.1.0
  • Reference

Write AquaCrop Soil Profile File

Source: R/write_sol.R
write_sol.Rd

Write an AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) soil profile (.SOL) file containing soil hydraulic properties and characteristics for each soil horizon. This file defines the soil water retention, hydraulic conductivity, and other physical properties used in the water balance calculations.

Usage

write_sol(
  path = "soil/",
  site_name = "default-site",
  cn = 46,
  rew = 5,
  texture = c("loam", "silt loam"),
  thickness = c(0.5, 1),
  eol = "windows"
)

Arguments

path

Directory path where the .SOL file will be written. Default = "soil/"

site_name

Name identifier for the soil profile (used in filename). Default = "default-site"

cn

Numeric. Soil curve number for runoff estimation (0-100). Default = 46

  • CN < 60: Low runoff potential (permeable soils, high infiltration)

  • CN 60-75: Moderate runoff potential

  • CN 75-85: High runoff potential

  • CN > 85: Very high runoff potential (impermeable surfaces)

rew

Numeric. Readily evaporable water from top layer (mm). Default = 5 Typical range: 4-12 mm depending on soil texture

texture

Character vector of soil texture names for each horizon. Must be from USDA classification. See SoilWater for valid textures. Default = c("loam", "silt loam")

thickness

Numeric vector of soil horizon thickness in meters. Must have same length as texture. Default = c(0.5, 1.0)

eol

End-of-line character style for the output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", or "macOS". Default = "windows"

Value

Invisibly returns the full path to the created .SOL file.

Details

The .SOL file contains soil profile information organized by horizons (layers). For each horizon, the following properties are specified from the SoilWater dataset:

Soil Hydraulic Properties:

  • SAT: Saturation point (vol%, 0 kPa)

  • FC: Field capacity (vol%, -33 kPa)

  • WP: Wilting point (vol%, -1500 kPa)

  • Ksat: Saturated hydraulic conductivity (mm/day)

Physical Properties:

  • Penetrability: Root zone expansion rate (%, typically 100)

  • Gravel: Volumetric gravel content (%)

  • CRa, CRb: Capillary rise coefficients (dimensionless)

Number of Horizons:

The number of soil horizons is automatically derived from the length of the texture and thickness vectors. Both must have the same length.

Curve Number (CN):

The CN value affects surface runoff generation. Choose based on:

  • Soil texture (clay soils have higher CN)

  • Soil structure and infiltration capacity

  • Surface conditions and vegetation cover

Initial Water Content:

This function only writes the soil profile characteristics (.SOL file). To specify initial water content conditions, use write_swo to create the corresponding .SW0 file separately.

See also

  • SoilWater for soil hydraulic properties data

  • write_swo for creating initial water content files (.SW0)

  • write_cro for creating crop files

  • write_man for creating management files

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
  # Simple: Single loam horizon
  write_sol(
    path = "soil/",
    site_name = "field-1",
    texture = "loam",
    thickness = 1.0
  )

  # Two horizons with different textures
  write_sol(
    path = "soil/",
    site_name = "layered-soil",
    cn = 65,
    rew = 7,
    texture = c("sandy loam", "clay loam"),
    thickness = c(0.4, 0.6)
  )

  # Three horizons
  write_sol(
    path = "soil/",
    site_name = "deep-profile",
    cn = 72,
    rew = 6,
    texture = c("loam", "clay loam", "clay"),
    thickness = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7)
  )

  # Complete workflow: SOL + SW0
  write_sol(
    site_name = "wakanda",
    texture = c("loam", "clay"),
    thickness = c(0.5, 1.0)
  )

  write_swo(
    soil_name = "wakanda",
    texture = c("loam", "clay"),
    layer_thickness = c(0.5, 1.0),
    initial_water = "FC"
  )
} # }
